checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=222101000 (2009-01-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=356796000 (2010-01-30), a 60.7% increase, while Revenues=2950563000 (FY2009) vs Revenues=2807687000 (FY2010), a -4.8% decline
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 balance sheet / income statement, XBRL facts

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=330238000 (2013-02-02) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=453496000 (2014-02-01), a +37.3% increase, while Revenues=3168630000 (FY2013) vs Revenues=3404400000 (FY2014), a +7.4% increase
evidence.location: 10-K FY2014/FY2015 balance sheet / income statement, XBRL facts

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=453496000 (2014-02-01) declined to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=420955000 (2015-01-31) even as Revenues grew from 3404400000 (FY2014) to 3706963000 (FY2015), indicating partial reversal

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=1997630000 (2009-01-31) essentially flat to Goodwill=2029945000 (2015-01-31), a cumulative ~1.6% increase over 6 years versus Revenues growth from 2950563000 (FY2009) to 3706963000 (FY2015), +25.6%
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015, Goodwill and Revenues XBRL facts

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=286534000 (2009-01-31) declined steadily to IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=27645000 (2015-05-02), consistent with ordinary amortization rather than capitalization buildup
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 FY2016, Intangible assets XBRL facts

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent ranged narrowly, e.g. OtherAssetsNoncurrent=138327000 (2009-01-31) to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=128839000 (2015-01-31), showing no outsized growth relative to Revenues growth over the same period
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 FY2016, Other assets noncurrent XBRL facts

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=147242000 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=680685000 (FY2009); NetIncomeLoss=904129000 (FY2011) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1193548000 (FY2011); NetIncomeLoss=315320000 (FY2014) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=448039000 (FY2014); NetIncomeLoss=435346000 (FY2015) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=728936000 (FY2015)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015, cash flow statement XBRL facts

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=250420000 (2013-02-02) grew to InventoryNet=347861000 (2014-02-01), a +38.9% increase, while Revenues grew only from 3168630000 (FY2013) to 3404400000 (FY2014), +7.4%
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015, Inventory and Revenues XBRL facts

evidence.quote: Gross margin computed from Revenues=3611893000 minus CostOfGoodsSold=1473274000 (FY2011, 59.2% margin) compressed to Revenues=3706963000 minus CostOfGoodsSold=1843706000 (FY2015, 50.3% margin), a ~9 point decline coincident with the inventory build-up
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015, Revenues and CostOfGoodsSold XBRL facts

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=347861000 (2014-02-01) declined to InventoryNet=308162000 (2015-01-31), an -11.4% decrease, while Revenues grew +8.9% over the same period, indicating partial normalization/possible write-down of the prior year build-up
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 FY2016 / 10-K FY2015, Inventory XBRL facts

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-114427000 (FY2008 annual) followed by NetIncomeLoss=147242000 (FY2009), NetIncomeLoss=353456000 (FY2010), NetIncomeLoss=904129000 (FY2011), NetIncomeLoss=615091000 (FY2012), NetIncomeLoss=306585000 (FY2013) show substantial period-to-period volatility, not unusual smoothing
Quarterly NetIncomeLoss swung from -111457000 (Q1 FY2010) to 201599000 (Q3 FY2010), tracking underlying Revenues volatility rather than smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=7366000 (2011-01-29) declined to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=2663000 (2012-01-28), a 63.9% reduction, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent only declined 11.4% (459406000 to 407263000) over the same period, lowering the reserve-to-AR ratio from ~1.60% to ~0.65%
evidence.location: 10-K FY2012, Allowance for doubtful accounts XBRL facts

evidence.quote: No AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent data is provided in the dataset for any period after 2012-01-28, despite AccountsReceivableNetCurrent continuing to be reported through 2015-05-02 (393814000)
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 FY2016, XBRL facts (absence of allowance tag)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filed 2006-09-07, followed by NT 10-Q/A filed 2006-09-28, NT 10-Q filed 2006-12-06, NT 10-K filed 2007-03-29, NT 10-Q filed 2007-06-08, and subsequently 10-K filed 2007-07-02, 10-Q/A filed 2007-07-02, 8-K/A filed 2007-07-02, and 10-K/A filed 2007-07-13

Additional amendment filings include 8-K/A on 2001-03-20, 8-K/A on 2006-01-13, 8-K/A on 2007-01-24, and 8-K/A on 2008-06-02, indicating a recurring pattern of corrective/late disclosures across multiple years, most concentrated around 2006-2007

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data for Revenues, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, NetIncomeLoss, and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities are available continuously from FY2008 (2008-02-02) through Q1 FY2016 (2015-05-02), providing over 7 years of trend data

AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent and detailed NetCashProvidedByUsedInFinancingActivities/InvestingActivities data are missing for periods after FY2012/FY2014 respectively, limiting some reserve and cash-flow-composition analysis in the most recent periods

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Historical understatement of stock-based compensation expense (options granted below fair value / backdating-type issue) requiring subsequent restatement, evidenced by clustering of NT 10-Q (2006-09-07, 2006-12-06, 2007-06-08), NT 10-K (2007-03-29), 10-Q/A (2007-07-02) and 10-K/A (2007-07-13) filings in the chronology, indicating the company could not timely file and later amended prior period financial statements.

rationale_evidence.quote: NT 10-K filed 2007-03-29 followed by 10-K filed 2007-07-02 and 10-K/A filed 2007-07-13

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible revenue pulled forward or extended customer credit terms (channel stuffing) inflating recognized revenue and receivables in FY2014, with AR growing far faster than revenue (a classic days-sales-outstanding expansion signal), partially reversing in FY2015.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=330238000 (2013-02-02) to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=453496000 (2014-02-01), +37.3%, vs Revenues +7.4% (3168630000 to 3404400000) over the same period
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent declined to 420955000 (2015-01-31) even as Revenues rose to 3706963000 (FY2015), suggesting the FY2014 receivables build was not sustained

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential delayed or insufficient inventory valuation reserves (obsolescence/lower-of-cost-or-market write-downs) as inventory grew far faster than revenue in FY2014 while gross margin compressed, consistent with excess/aging inventory not being fully reserved until later periods.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet=250420000 (2013-02-02) grew to InventoryNet=347861000 (2014-02-01), +38.9%, vs Revenues +7.4% growth
Gross margin fell from 59.2% (FY2011: Revenues 3611893000, CostOfGoodsSold 1473274000) to 50.3% (FY2015: Revenues 3706963000, CostOfGoodsSold 1843706000)

top_signals: Historical filing irregularities in 2006-2007 (multiple NT 10-Q/NT 10-K, 10-Q/A, 10-K/A, 8-K/A) indicate a documented past restatement episode
FY2014 receivables grew ~37% versus 7.4% revenue growth, then partially reversed in FY2015
FY2014 inventory grew ~39% versus 7.4% revenue growth coincident with multi-year gross margin compression (59.2%→50.3%)
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined disproportionately relative to receivables in FY2011-FY2012 and disclosure of this reserve ceases thereafter in the dataset
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income in every year examined, which is a mitigating/positive quality signal
